arguments.name: explore-aot
arguments.prompt:
Explore the project in /tmp/sv-73b741f4bfce44949b91222c67d1c836/before/ to understand what needs to change for AOT (Ahead-of-Time) compilation compatibility in .NET.

Specifically:
1. Read the .csproj file to check current AOT settings
2. Look at all source files that use reflection, JSON serialization, or dynamic types that would break under AOT
3. Check for uses of `JsonSerializer`, `JsonSerializerOptions`, `JsonSerializerContext`, or any System.Text.Json usage patterns
4. Check for `[JsonSerializable]` attributes or lack thereof
5. Look for `IUtf8JsonSerializable`, `IJsonModel`, `IPersistableModel` interfaces and their implementations
6. Check if there's a `BicepModelReaderWriterOptions.cs` and see if it has any reflection usage
7. Look for any `TypeFormatters`, `ManagedServiceIdentityTypeV3Converter`, or custom JSON converters
8. Check for `[DynamicallyAccessedMembers]` or `[RequiresUnreferencedCode]` annotations
9. Look at the `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` for any AOT-related assembly attributes
10. Search for any `JsonConverter` subclasses
11. Check if there are any generated model classes with serialization code (under Resources/Generated, ManagementGroup/Generated, Common/Generated)
12. Look for usage of `Type.GetType`, `Activator.CreateInstance`, `Assembly.Load`, `MakeGenericType`, or other reflection APIs
13. Check the global.json and Directory.Build.props for any relevant settings

Report ALL files and their content that would need changes for AOT compatibility. Be thorough - list every file path and the relevant code sections.
